target_gene_count_6640.text: - Replaced parent's bilinear head (gene_class_emb [3, 6640, 256]) with linear head, losing inductive bias for mapping STRING_GNN embeddings to gene-specific logits
- Restored STRING-only architecture with frozen STRING_GNN embeddings and bilinear gene-class embeddings [3, 6640, 256]
- MLP head dimension changed: parent used direct GenePriorBias, this uses MLP [256] → bilinear output [3, 6640]
- GenePriorBias [3, 6640] with 40-epoch warmup via persistent register_buffer
- Added GenePriorBias module: learnable [3, 6640] per-gene calibration bias with 50-epoch gradient warmup via persistent register_buffer
- GenePriorBias ([3, 6640], warmup=50 epochs via register_buffer)
- Added GenePriorBias calibration ([3, 6640] learnable bias with 50-epoch warmup via persistent register_buffer)
- Implemented bilinear gene-class head producing [B, 3, 6640] logits
- Architecture: from-scratch bilinear MLP baseline (gene embedding 512-dim → MLP 256→128 → bilinear head [3,6640,128])
- Lack of pretrained genomic knowledge: random embeddings for 1,542 perturbations cannot capture gene regulatory relationships needed to predict 6,640-gene differential expression patterns
- Added GenePriorBias module: [3, 6640] learnable per-gene output calibration, frozen for 30 epochs then activated
- Added GenePriorBias: learnable [3, 6640] per-gene calibration bias
- Over-regularized classification head: head_hidden=256 with dropout=0.5 and weight_decay=0.05 catastrophically restrictive for 6,640-gene classification task with 1,388 training samples
- Replaced concatenation fusion with bilinear gene-class embedding head (gene_class_emb [3, 6640, 256], 5.1M params)
- GenePriorBias [3, 6640] (learnable per-gene per-class additive bias initialized from training label log-frequencies, zero-meaned per gene)
- Removed GenePriorBias module ([3, 6640] per-gene class log-prior with 20-epoch warmup) that distorted early loss landscape
- GenePriorBias module: [3, 6640] per-gene class log-prior with 20-epoch gradient warmup
- Architecture: Added GenePriorBias ([3,6640] additive head bias)
- Added GenePriorBias module: 3×6640=19,920 params, initialized from training log-class-frequencies
- Gated cross-attention fusion module for 3-class differential expression prediction (6,640 genes per perturbation)

training_samples_1388.text: - Concatenating heterogeneous [256] STRING + [640] AIDO embeddings creates confused feature space for linear head with only 1,388 samples
- Severe overfitting from oversized head (bilinear_dim=512 + 2 ResBlocks on 1,388 samples)
- Excessive fine-tuning capacity (42.5M params) on only 1,388 samples caused overfitting/noise
- Excessive model capacity: 5.1M parameters in gene_class_emb alone for 1,388 samples
- Model must learn protein-to-transcriptional-response mapping from limited 1,388 training samples
- Unfreezing STRING_GNN backbone adds capacity that overfits on 1,388 training samples
- Discriminative backbone LR failed on small dataset (1,388 samples) due to noisy full-graph forward passes
- Primary: LoRA r=16 (~0.55M extra trainable params) creates harder optimization problem with 1,388 samples, offsetting expected +0.015–0.025 discriminative LR benefit
- Training samples: 1,388
- Dataset: 1,388 samples
- Severe overfitting from doubling LoRA rank: trainable parameters increased from ~0.55M to ~1.1M on 1,388 training samples
- Discriminative learning rate (head_lr=4× backbone_lr) caused 5.3M-param head to overfit 1,388-sample training set while under-training AIDO.Cell backbone
- No data augmentation for small dataset (n=1,388 samples)
- Difficult optimization problem with only 1,388 training samples
- Fast-converging head produced sharper local minimum on 1,388 samples; slower backbone unable to compensate within training budget
- Persistent val F1 plateau with widening train-val gap indicates overfitting on 1,388 samples
- Over-regularized classification head: head_hidden=256 with dropout=0.5 and weight_decay=0.05 catastrophically restrictive for 6,640-gene classification task with 1,388 training samples
- Large classification head (10.9M params) on only 1,388 training samples — primary overfitting driver
- Model params: 10.7M classification head on 1,388 samples
- Large classification head (10.7M params on 1,388 samples) drives persistent overfitting

perturbation_count_1542.text: - Lack of pretrained genomic knowledge: random embeddings for 1,542 perturbations cannot capture gene regulatory relationships needed to predict 6,640-gene differential expression patterns

aido.text: - Added AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA r=8 for transcriptome signal fusion
- Concatenating heterogeneous [256] STRING + [640] AIDO embeddings creates confused feature space for linear head with only 1,388 samples
- AIDO.Cell fusion via raw concatenation cannot compensate for lost bilinear mapping despite transcriptome signal
- Removed failed AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA fusion that caused parent's catastrophic collapse (F1 0.4123 → 0.4712, +0.059)
- Added AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA (r=8) to frozen STRING_GNN backbone
- Fusion: simple concatenation of STRING_GNN [128-dim] + AIDO.Cell embeddings [768-dim] → 896-dim
- **Architectural mismatch**: simple concatenation fusion failed to leverage AIDO.Cell signals effectively; the fusion approach did not complement the frozen STRING_GNN backbone
- Tree best node (node2-1-1-1-1) achieves F1=0.5128 via AIDO.Cell-100M/scFoundation LoRA fine-tuning
- Frozen STRING_GNN backbone lacks transcriptional perturbation-aware representations compared to AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA + STRING_GNN fusion (F1=0.5128)
- Added AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA encoder (r=8, α=16, target QKV in all 18 transformer layers, 640-dim output)
- Architecture: frozen STRING_GNN K=16 2-head neighborhood attention (256-dim) → concat with AIDO.Cell LoRA (640-dim) → 896-dim → 2-layer MLP head (896→256→19920)

- GenePriorBias interferes with AIDO.Cell LoRA fine-tuning rather than acting orthogonally — simultaneous training creates conflicting gradient signals, depressing performance by ~0.028 relative to node2-1-1-1-1-1 baseline
- GenePriorBias corrupts learned representations mid-training (identical failure mode observed across all nodes using it in both STRING_GNN and AIDO.Cell lineages)
- AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA (r=16) fusion added to STRING_GNN backbone (vs frozen STRING_GNN-only)
- Cross-node evidence: 4 AIDO.Cell fusion nodes with weighted CE achieved F1 > 0.50 (mean 0.507) vs focal loss node at 0.454
- STRING_GNN-only architecture appears to have performance ceiling ~0.47-0.48, below best overall node (AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA: 0.5128)
- Simplified to proven 2-backbone fusion: AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA + STRING_GNN K=16
- Triple-backbone fusion: AIDO.Cell-100M (LoRA r=8) + STRING_GNN (K=16, 2-head neighborhood attention) + ESM2-650M (3840→256 projected)
- Added AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA r=8 (553K trainable params) as gene expression encoder
- Replaced bilinear head with 2-layer MLP fusion for AIDO.Cell + STRING_GNN embeddings

scfoundation.text: - Tree best node (node2-1-1-1-1) achieves F1=0.5128 via AIDO.Cell-100M/scFoundation LoRA fine-tuning
- Switched from fine-tuned scFoundation (42.5M params) to pre-computed frozen scFoundation buffers
- Attempted to fix parent's scFoundation fine-tuning failure via frozen approach
- Frozen scFoundation embeddings from single-token perturbation-identity input produce near-zero-variance outputs
- Frozen scFoundation dominates GatedFusion input (768 > 512) and suppresses STRING+ESM2 signal
- Frozen scFoundation fusion hypothesis falsified for 5th time across search tree
- Triple-backbone architecture: added fine-tuned scFoundation (top-6 genes) to frozen STRING_GNN + frozen ESM2-650M
- Trainable parameters: 42.5M from scFoundation fine-tuning
- scFoundation produced near-zero-value embeddings for single-token perturbation-identity inputs (degenerate per-sample representation)
- GatedFusion suppressed scFoundation pathway, effectively reverting to parent's dual-backbone performance
- Replaced discriminative LR STRING_GNN backbone with frozen STRING_GNN + frozen scFoundation (100M) gated fusion architecture
- scFoundation fusion failed to provide complementary signal to STRING_GNN
- 1-hot perturbation encoding (19,264 genes) misaligned with scFoundation's dense expression pretraining
- Gated fusion likely suppresses STRING_GNN signal or amplifies noisy scFoundation embeddings
- Replaced frozen scFoundation fusion with GenePriorBias calibration module (per-gene per-class additive bias, 50-epoch gradient warmup)
- Fused frozen pre-computed scFoundation single-gene perturbation embeddings (768-dim) with frozen STRING_GNN via 2-layer MLP fusion head (1024→512→256)
- Frozen scFoundation embeddings (pretrained on steady-state gene expression, encoding genes as 1-hot expression=1.0 vectors) provide zero perturbation-specific transcriptional signal
- scFoundation introduces orthogonal noise that degrades STRING_GNN's PPI topology information
- Frozen scFoundation fusion hypothesis falsified across 4 independent experiments (node1-3, node1-3-1, node1-1-2-1-1, node1-2-2), all performing below their STRING-only parents

- All other architecture/hyperparameters remained identical to parent (scFoundation top-6 layers fine-tuned, frozen STRING_GNN, GatedFusion, GenePriorBias, mixup alpha=0.2, cosine annealing with min_lr_ratio=0.05)

string_gnn.text: - Replaced parent's bilinear head (gene_class_emb [3, 6640, 256]) with linear head, losing inductive bias for mapping STRING_GNN embeddings to gene-specific logits
- Concatenating heterogeneous [256] STRING + [640] AIDO embeddings creates confused feature space for linear head with only 1,388 samples
- Aggregation weighted by STRING edge confidence with center-context gating
- Static STRING_GNN embeddings lack perturbation-specific transcriptional response signal (fundamental bottleneck)
- Restored STRING-only architecture with frozen STRING_GNN embeddings and bilinear gene-class embeddings [3, 6640, 256]
- GenePriorBias was never activated — bias_activation_epoch=80 scheduled 3 epochs after EarlyStopping fired at patience=25, so entire training was effectively STRING-only without bias calibration
- Embedding noise augmentation (σ=0.05) disrupted STRING topology signal without regularization benefit
- Added AIDO.Cell-100M with LoRA (r=8) to frozen STRING_GNN backbone
- Fusion: simple concatenation of STRING_GNN [128-dim] + AIDO.Cell embeddings [768-dim] → 896-dim
- **Architectural mismatch**: simple concatenation fusion failed to leverage AIDO.Cell signals effectively; the fusion approach did not complement the frozen STRING_GNN backbone
- STRING_GNN-only ceiling (~0.49) — frozen PPI topology cannot capture perturbation-specific gene expression signals
- STRING-only ceiling at ~0.485 is the binding constraint (frozen STRING_GNN encodes static PPI topology without perturbation-specific signal)
- Frozen STRING_GNN backbone lacks transcriptional perturbation-aware representations compared to AIDO.Cell-100M LoRA + STRING_GNN fusion (F1=0.5128)
- Architecture: frozen STRING_GNN K=16 2-head neighborhood attention (256-dim) → concat with AIDO.Cell LoRA (640-dim) → 896-dim → 2-layer MLP head (896→256→19920)
- Maintained frozen STRING_GNN backbone with bilinear gene_class_emb (5.1M params)
- Test score 0.4846 exactly matches best STRING-only result in lineage — confirms plateau for frozen STRING_GNN approaches
- Frozen STRING_GNN encodes static PPI topology without perturbation-specific signal, capping performance at ~0.485
- Frozen STRING_GNN encodes static PPI topology without perturbation-specific signal
- Frozen STRING_GNN bilinear MLP (bilinear_dim=256, dropout=0.4, lr=2e-4)
- Mixup on frozen STRING_GNN embeddings: PPI embedding space encodes discrete graph topology, not continuous manifolds; convex combinations produce meaningless virtual perturbations

data_files.text: - val.tsv and test.tsv contain identical 154 samples (val/f1 should equal test/f1 when computed correctly)

best_node.memory_excerpt:
**Improvements from Parent**
- Reduced STRING_GNN neighborhood size from K=24 to K=16
- Simplified MLP head from 3-layer (896→512→256→19920) to 2-layer (896→256→19920)
- Increased head dropout from 0.4 to 0.5 for stronger regularization
- Maintained multi-head (2-head) neighborhood attention architecture

**Results & Metrics (vs Parent)**
- Test/f1: 0.5128 (vs parent 0.4471) — **+0.066 improvement**
- Best val/f1: 0.5128 at epoch 77 (vs parent val/f1=0.486)
- Generalization gap: 0.0 (val/f1 = test/f1 exactly)
- Training epochs: 88 (early stopped)
- vs grandparent (node2-1-1-1): +0.007 test/f1 improvement

**Key Issues**
- Checkpoint saved at epoch 33 (val/f1=0.4905), not at true best epoch 77 (val/f1=0.5128)
- Patience=10 barely sufficient to capture late-improvement spike at epoch 77
- Best performance checkpoint missed by 44 epochs
best_node.code_header_excerpt:
"""Node2-1-1-1-1-1: AIDO.Cell-100M (LoRA) + STRING_GNN K=16 Multi-Head (2-head) Neighborhood Attention + 2-Layer Head

Targeted improvements over parent node2-1-1-1-1 (K=24 multi-head + 3-layer head, F1=0.4471):

ROOT CAUSE FIXES (addressing parent's -0.059 regression):
1. Revert K: 24 → 16   — removes low-confidence noisy PPI neighbors (rank 17-24)
2. Revert head: 3-layer (896→512→256→19920) → 2-layer (896→256→19920) — removes overfitting
3. Restore head_dropout: 0.4 → 0.5 — restore proven regularization from grandparent
4. Restore patience: 15 → 10 — appropriate stopping for stable training

RETAINED IMPROVEMENT from parent:
5. Keep multi-head attention (2 heads, K=16) — test multi-head in isolation as recommended
   by feedback: "First, test K=16 multi-head (2-head) vs K=16 single-head in isolation"

NEW IMPROVEMENTS over grandparent (node2-1-1-1, F1=0.5059):
6. max_epochs: 200 → 300 — grandparent training wasn't converged at epoch 63/200
7. patience: 7 → 10 — allows cosine annealing to converge fully in extended training

This node is designed as a clean incremental test: "can 2-head attention at K=16 improve
over 1-head at K=16, while maintaining all proven regularization?"
"""

import os
os.environ['PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF'] = 'expandable_segments:True'

import argparse
import json
from datetime import timedelta
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F
import lightning.pytorch as pl
from lightning.pytorch.callbacks import EarlyStopping, LearningRateMonitor, ModelCheckpoint
from lightning.pytorch.callbacks.progress import TQDMProgressBar
from lightning.pytorch.loggers import CSVLogger, TensorBoardLogger
from lightning.pytorch.strategies import DDPStrategy
from peft import LoraConfig, TaskType, get_peft_model
from torch.optim.lr_scheduler import CosineAnnealingLR, LinearLR, SequentialLR
from torch.utils.data import DataLoader, Dataset
from transformers import AutoModel, AutoTokenizer

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Constants
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
N_GENES          = 6640
N_CLASSES        = 3
AIDO_GENES       = 19264       # AIDO.Cell gene vocabulary size
AIDO_MODEL_DIR   = "/home/Models/AIDO.Cell-100M"
STRING_MODEL_DIR = "/home/Models/STRING_GNN"
AIDO_HIDDEN      = 640         # AIDO.Cell-100M hidden size
STRING_HIDDEN    = 256         # STRING_GNN hidden size
NEIGHBOR_K       = 16          # REVERTED to 16 (from 2

k562_concat.target_gene_selection: top 1000 genes by ncells then ncounts among expressed genes
k562_concat.label_rule: per perturbation, delta vs control mean expression; <=5th percentile down, >=95th percentile up, otherwise unchanged
k562_concat.feature_description: Concatenated perturbation one-hot and z-scored mean expression delta features.

k562_concat_esm2_gene_embedding.target_gene_selection: top 1000 genes by ncells then ncounts among expressed genes
k562_concat_esm2_gene_embedding.label_rule: per perturbation, delta vs control mean expression; <=5th percentile down, >=95th percentile up, otherwise unchanged
k562_concat_esm2_gene_embedding.feature_description: source_plus_embedding using real gene embedding artifact ESM2_D1280; perturbation gene symbols aligned to obs/symbol.

inferred_alignment_gaps: Official visible K562 code uses N_GENES=6640; current repo K562 manifests use n_target_genes=1000.
Official memory repeatedly cites 1,388 training samples; current repo uses train/val/test perturbation rows 73/16/16.
Official best node uses AIDO.Cell-100M and STRING_GNN model directories; current repo has ESM2/STRING/Reactome artifacts but no AIDO.Cell checkpoint or official STRING_GNN checkpoint.
Official code expects train.tsv/val.tsv/test.tsv with labels per perturbation row; public VCHarness tree inspected here does not include those TSV data files.
Official metric implementation is per-gene macro F1 over 3 classes and 6,640 genes; current repo reports Macro-F1 on a 1,000-gene local label universe.
